Title: Morgan Stanley Says The US Midterm Elections Could Spark A Rally In Stocks: Here's How To Trade It 
 Content: ['Hawkish comments from the U.S. Federal Reserve weighed on markets last week. With midterm elections and more inflation data on the horizon, markets will be tested once again, but one analyst firm told\xa0investors to stay bullish on equities.\xa0', "What To Know: Morgan Stanley's\xa0Michael Wilson\xa0was\xa0staying bullish\xa0this week as he anticipated\xa0the midterm elections acting as a catalyst for stocks.", '"In the end, we think it survives again with the midterms providing the catalyst for lower bond yields and higher equity prices," Wilson wrote Monday in a\xa0note to clients.\xa0', "With polls pointed\xa0to Republicans winning at least one chamber of Congress, Wilson expects\xa0the midterms to throw a wrench\xa0in\xa0the Democrat's aggressive fiscal spending plans. The Republicans have talked about freezing debt spending via the debt ceiling as they did in 2011, he noted.", '"This would be a sharp reversal from the past few years when budget deficits reached levels not seen since World War II. In our view, a clean sweep by the Republicans on Tuesday could greatly raise the odds of such an outcome," Wilson said.\xa0', 'He anticipated\xa0a clean sweep\xa0for Republicans would spark a market rally, but he warned the results may not be clear until later in the week.', '', 'See Also:\xa0US Midterms Susceptible To Violence As YouTube May Have False Information, Researchers Warn', 'How To Trade It: Given the delay in counting mail-in ballots, Wilson anticipated\xa0elevated\xa0volatility in the markets and recommended\xa0tight stop losses be put in place.', '"Because this is purely a tactical trading view and not in line with our core fundamental view which remains bearish, we will remain disciplined on how much leash to give it," Wilson said.\xa0', 'Last week, the\xa0SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trus\xa0SPY traded down to\xa0the $370\xa0level before moving higher. Morgan Stanley believed\xa0that level could be tested again this week, but it was\xa0willing to stay in bullish trades all the way down to the $365 level.\xa0', '"Anxiety around the CPI release scheduled for Thursday morning is another reason to think both rate and equity vol will remain high," Wilson said.', 'The analyst firm noted the 10-Year Treasury yield was a better\xa0trading signal this week. If it made\xa0new highs above 4.35%, Morgan Stanley said it would advise clients to exit bullish trades. Still, the firm sees a potential trading opportunity ahead.', '"In short, the bear market rally is likely to hang around for longer than most expect if it can survive this week\'s test," Wilson said.', 'SPY Price Action: The SPY was up 0.22% at $377.19\xa0at the time of publication, according to\xa0Benzinga Pro.', 'Photo:\xa0Angelique Johnson\xa0from Pixabay.', '© 2023 Benzinga.com. Benzinga does not provide investment advice. All rights reserved.']

 Content: ['Hawkish comments from the U.S. Federal Reserve weighed on markets last week. With midterm elections and more inflation data on the horizon, markets will be tested once again, but one analyst firm told\xa0investors to stay bullish on equities.\xa0', "What To Know: Morgan Stanley's\xa0Michael Wilson\xa0was\xa0staying bullish\xa0this week as he anticipated\xa0the midterm elections acting as a catalyst for stocks.", '"In the end, we think it survives again with the midterms providing the catalyst for lower bond yields and higher equity prices," Wilson wrote Monday in a\xa0note to clients.\xa0', "With polls pointed\xa0to Republicans winning at least one chamber of Congress, Wilson expects\xa0the midterms to throw a wrench\xa0in\xa0the Democrat's aggressive fiscal spending plans. The Republicans have talked about freezing debt spending via the debt ceiling as they did in 2011, he noted.", '"This would be a sharp reversal from the past few years when budget deficits reached levels not seen since World War II. In our view, a clean sweep by the Republicans on Tuesday could greatly raise the odds of such an outcome," Wilson said.\xa0', 'He anticipated\xa0a clean sweep\xa0for Republicans would spark a market rally, but he warned the results may not be clear until later in the week.', '', 'See Also:\xa0US Midterms Susceptible To Violence As YouTube May Have False Information, Researchers Warn', 'How To Trade It: Given the delay in counting mail-in ballots, Wilson anticipated\xa0elevated\xa0volatility in the markets and recommended\xa0tight stop losses be put in place.', '"Because this is purely a tactical trading view and not in line with our core fundamental view which remains bearish, we will remain disciplined on how much leash to give it," Wilson said.\xa0', 'Last week, the\xa0SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trus\xa0SPY traded down to\xa0the $370\xa0level before moving higher. Morgan Stanley believed\xa0that level could be tested again this week, but it was\xa0willing to stay in bullish trades all the way down to the $365 level.\xa0', '"Anxiety around the CPI release scheduled for Thursday morning is another reason to think both rate and equity vol will remain high," Wilson said.', 'The analyst firm noted the 10-Year Treasury yield was a better\xa0trading signal this week. If it made\xa0new highs above 4.35%, Morgan Stanley said it would advise clients to exit bullish trades. Still, the firm sees a potential trading opportunity ahead.', '"In short, the bear market rally is likely to hang around for longer than most expect if it can survive this week\'s test," Wilson said.', 'SPY Price Action: The SPY was up 0.22% at $377.19\xa0at the time of publication, according to\xa0Benzinga Pro.', 'Photo:\xa0Angelique Johnson\xa0from Pixabay.', '© 2023 Benzinga.com. Benzinga does not provide investment advice. All rights reserved.']
